decisions.reasoning: Incineroar will likely pivot or use Fake Out; U-turn allows me to deal chip damage while maintaining momentum and bringing in a better check to Incineroar.
Incineroar provides an Intimidate drop to mitigate physical pressure and offers a safe pivot against the opponent's Incineroar, preserving Tornadus-Therian's health for later use.
Calyrex-Ice is at 41% and will be outsped and KOed by Flare Blitz next turn. Switching to Tornadus-Therian allows me to preserve Calyrex-Ice for later while checking Incineroar's momentum.

Tornadus-Therian is at 46% HP and will be KO'd by Flare Blitz next turn. Switching to Incineroar allows me to utilize Intimidate to mitigate incoming physical damage and pivot safely while preserving Tornadus for a potential Regenerator recovery later.
Calyrex-Ice has fainted, so I must send in a new Pokemon. Tornadus-Therian is a safe pivot that resists Incineroar's Flare Blitz and can threaten it with momentum or chip damage.

Tornadus-Therian outspeeds Incineroar and Hurricane deals significant chip damage (45-53%). Since Incineroar's Flare Blitz is a 2HKO and I am at 80% HP, I can safely attack to force progress before pivoting out.
Tornadus-Therian is at 29% and will be KO'd by Flare Blitz, so switching to Flutter Mane preserves Tornadus's Regenerator ability for later while bringing in a faster threat.
Flutter Mane has fainted, so I must send in a new Pokemon. Incineroar is the best choice to pivot into the opponent's Incineroar to neutralize its attack with Intimidate and scout its next move.
Incineroar is at 29% HP, and Hurricane is guaranteed to KO it this turn. Since I outspeed, I should take the guaranteed KO rather than switching.
Hurricane is a guaranteed OHKO on Urshifu-Rapid-Strike, and since I outspeed, I should take the KO immediately to remove the threat.
